user_message: David Rivera at Fourth Coffee just sent this. Help me prep a brief.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 14:42 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 09:11 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Fourth Coffee

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-I trap (MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-I failure mode (MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-I -- MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness
